description: city in Washtenaw County, Michigan, United States
description_wiki: Ypsilanti ( IP-sə-LAN-tee), commonly shortened to Ypsi ( IP-see), is a city and college town located on the Huron River in Washtenaw County, Michigan, United States.

description: public research university in Urbana and Champaign, Illinois, United States

description_wiki: Piatt County is a county in  Illinois.
World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: public research university in West Lafayette, Indiana, United States
description_wiki: Purdue University is a public land-grant research university in West Lafayette, Indiana, United States, and the flagship campus of the Purdue University system, which also includes Purdue University in Indianapolis as an ongoing expansion of the West Lafayette campus.

aliases: University of Notre Dame du Lac
University of Notre Dame, South Bend
description: Catholic university located in Notre Dame, Indiana, United States
description_wiki: The University of Notre Dame du Lac (known simply as Notre Dame;   NOH-tər-DAYM; ND) is a private Catholic research university in Notre Dame, Indiana, United States.

description: city in and the county seat of Vermilion County, Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Danville is a city in Vermilion County, Illinois, United States, and its county seat.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.
Henry Wadsworth Longfellow (February 27, 1807 – March 24, 1882) was an American poet and educator.

description: estuary in the U.S. states of Maryland, Delaware, District of Columbia, and Virginia
description_wiki: The Chesapeake Bay ( CHESS-ə-peek) is the largest estuary in the United States.

description: independent city in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Norfolk (locally   NOR-fək) is an independent city in the U.S. commonwealth of Virginia.

description: capital and largest city of Massachusetts, United States
description_wiki: Boston () is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Massachusetts.

description: landing ship designed for carrying armored vehicles and troops
city in and county seat of Honolulu County, Hawaii, United States, that is also the capital city of Hawaii
description_wiki: Honolulu (  HON-ə-LOO-loo; Hawaiian: [honoˈlulu]) is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Hawaii, located in the Pacific Ocean.

description: world War II Bases in Territory of Hawaii
description_wiki: Naval Base Hawaii  was a number of United States Navy bases in the Territory of Hawaii during World War II. At the start of the war, much of the Hawaiian Islands was converted from tourism to a United States Armed Forces base.

aliases: United States Naval Station Guantanamo Bay
description: military base of the United States Navy
description_wiki: Guantanamo Bay Naval Base (Spanish: Base Naval de la Bahía de Guantánamo), officially known as Naval Station Guantanamo Bay or NSGB, (also called GTMO, pronounced Gitmo  GIT-moh as jargon by the U.S. military) is a United States military base located on 45 square miles (117 km2) of land and water on the shore of Guantánamo Bay at the southeastern end of Cuba.
The Panama Canal (Spanish: Canal de Panamá) is an artificial 82-kilometer (51-mile) waterway in Panama that connects the Caribbean Sea with the Pacific Ocean.

description: harbor on the island of Oahu, Hawaii, USA
description_wiki: Pearl Harbor is a lagoon harbor on the island of Oʻahu, Hawaii, United States, west of Honolulu.
